data.title: EXCLUSIVE: Nuvve Expands Energy Storage Push With OMNIA Global Deal
data.description:
Clean energy company Nuvve Holding Corp. (NASDAQ:NVVE) announced Friday a partnership with OMNIA Global to develop more than 1 gigawatt of battery energy storage projects over the next two years.

As part of this collaboration, the initiative will begin with a large-scale installation in Sweden. The agreement strengthens Nuvve's presence in Europe's rapidly expanding grid-scale energy storage sector.

Battery Storage Project Launch

The partnership's first project is a 50-MW/75-MWh CE-approved battery energy storage system in Sweden, expected to begin operations in Q2 2026.

BESS helps stabilize electricity networks by storing surplus power and delivering it during high demand. The technology is increasingly critical as renewable generation grows and grids require flexible capacity to manage fluctuations.

Market Opportunity And Revenue Potential

Sweden has become a key destination for storage investments due to strong renewable generation, expanding electrification across transportation and industry, and supportive energy market structures.

These factors are driving demand for grid-support solutions like battery storage.

Energy storage assets in some European markets are currently generating estimated annual revenue between 240,000 euros and 300,000 euros per megawatt (about $260,000–$325,000 per MW). Once operational, the Swedish facility could begin contributing incremental revenue to Nuvve in 2026

Nuvve will own and manage the facility while providing market access services to optimize revenue and support grid participation.

The move strengthens the company's position in the growing Nordic energy storage market. It also aligns with its long-term strategy to deploy scalable, revenue-generating infrastructure across expanding battery storage opportunities.

Executives Highlight Growth Opportunity

"The partnership represents a significant revenue opportunity spread across a pipeline exceeding 1GW, positioning Nuvve as a leading global provider of grid-scale energy storage," said Gregory Poilasne, CEO of Nuvve.

Daniel Hansen, CEO and chairman of OMNIA Global, commented, “The European market has high arbitrage opportunities for fast movers and with the partnership of Nuvve we have found a partner that efficiently and comfortably can look at a large scale roll out with the most experienced team of its sector."

NVVE Price Action: Nuvve Holding shares were up 2.40% at $1.28 during premarket trading on Friday. The stock is trading near its 52-week low of $1.00, according to Benzinga Pro data.

Photo by Es sarawuth via Shutterstock

data.title: Gap Shares Fall After Double Miss Despite Comparable Sales Growth
data.description:
Gap, Inc. (NYSE:GAP) shares are trading lower Friday after the company reported worse-than-expected fourth-quarter financial results on Thursday after the market closed.

  • Gap stock is showing notable weakness. What’s pressuring GAP stock?

Double Miss As Comparable Sales Grow

Gap reported earnings per share of 45 cents, missing the consensus estimate of 46 cents. In addition, the company reported revenue of $4.23 billion, missing the consensus estimate of $4.24 billion.

Comparable sales increased 3%, marking the company's eighth consecutive quarter of positive comparable sales. Online sales increased 5% from last year and represented 42% of total net sales.

"I am pleased to report that Gap Inc. delivered a successful fourth quarter, marking another year of meaningful progress," said President and CEO Richard Dickson.

Old Navy reported net sales of $2.3 billion, up 3% year over year with comparable sales also rising 3%. Gap brand net sales totaled $1.1 billion, increasing 8% from last year with comparable sales up 7%. Banana Republic posted net sales of $549 million, up 1% year over year with comparable sales rising 4%. Athleta reported net sales of $354 million, down 11% from last year with comparable sales declining 10%.

Gap ended the year with $3.0 billion in cash, cash equivalents and short-term investments.

The company also announced that its board approved a new $1 billion share repurchase authorization for its common stock. Gap said it will pay a first-quarter fiscal 2026 dividend of $0.175 per share, representing about a 6% increase from the prior quarterly dividend.

Looking ahead, the company sees fiscal-year adjusted earnings per share of $2.20 to $2.35, versus the consensus estimate of $2.32. Furthermore, it expects revenue of $15.70 billion to $15.86 billion, versus the consensus estimate of $15.75 billion.

For the first-quarter, the company anticipates revenue of $3.53 billion to $3.57 billion, versus the consensus estimate of $3.53 billion.

Gap Shares Fall

GAP Price Action: At the time of publication, Gap shares are trading 9.52% lower at $24.61, according to data from Benzinga Pro.

Image via Shutterstock

data.title: What's Going On With Nano Labs Shares On Friday?
data.description:
Shares of Nano Labs Ltd (NASDAQ:NA) fell in Friday's premarket session following the company's latest product launch.

The broader market also moved lower with declines in the S&P 500 and Nasdaq Composite.

Details

Nano Labs on Friday unveiled the iPollo ClawPC A1 Mini, a hardware device designed for the OpenClaw AI Agent System.

The product aims to support gaming, professional content creation, and smart office environments through localized AI-powered functionality. The launch is part of Nano Labs' broader push to expand its Web 3.0 infrastructure offerings and deepen its focus on high-performance computing and AI-driven applications.

The company also plans to introduce the iPollo Claw OS, intended to further integrate its products across digital ecosystems.

Chairman and CEO Jianping Kong said the launch represents a step forward in integrating AI software and hardware and reflects the company's vision for the emerging Web 4.0 era, where AI evolves from a supporting tool into an independent and collaborative digital entity.

Technical Analysis

Over the past 12 months, Nano Labs has seen its shares decline by 46.85%, reflecting broader market challenges and internal hurdles.

Currently, the stock is trading at its 52-week low of $2.75, significantly below its high of $31.48. This positioning near the lower end of its yearly range highlights the substantial volatility and downward pressure the stock has faced.

Technical indicators show mixed signals. The Relative Strength Index (RSI) is at 42.70, indicating a neutral market momentum, while the Moving Average Convergence Divergence (MACD) presents a bullish sign, with the MACD line at -0.0992 above the signal line at -0.1053.

These indicators suggest potential for near-term improvement, but the stock remains well below its key moving averages, signaling continued bearish trends.

Earnings & Analyst Outlook

Nano Labs is slated to provide its next financial update on March 24, 2026. With the earnings date approaching, investors are keen to see how recent product launches and market expansions are reflected in the company’s financials.

  • EPS Estimate: Loss of 84 cents (Down from 75 cents YoY)
  • Revenue Estimate: $2.21 million (Up from $2.00 million YoY)

Analyst Consensus & Recent Actions: The stock carries a N/A Rating. No recent analyst actions available.

Benzinga Edge Rankings

Below is the Benzinga Edge scorecard for Nano Labs, highlighting its strengths and weaknesses compared to the broader market:

  • Momentum: Weak (Score: 3.44) — The stock shows very weak performance indicators, reflecting its significant challenges in the market.

The Verdict: Nano Labs Ltd Class A Ordinary Shares’s Benzinga Edge signal reveals a challenging market position. The low Momentum score indicates significant hurdles ahead, although the recent product launch could provide a basis for recovery if successfully integrated into the market.

NA Price Action: Nano Labs shares were down 5.76% at $2.78 during premarket trading on Friday, according to Benzinga Pro data.

Photo via Shutterstock

data.title: Why Is Omada Health Stock Soaring Friday?
data.description:
Omada Health Inc. (NASDAQ:OMDA) shares are up in Friday’s premarket session following the announcement of its new GLP-1 Flex Care program, which aims to help employers provide access to GLP-1 medications for obesity.

The company on Thursday reported fourth-quarter results that beat expectations and issued upbeat guidance for 2026.

Employer GLP-1 Support

Omada Health’s GLP-1 Flex Care offering enables employers to manage the costs of GLP-1 medications while providing necessary clinical oversight.

The program, announced on Thursday, aims to address the growing demand among employees for these medications, which have seen notable increases in persistence rates among users, with 67% remaining on medication after one year, compared to 47-49% in similar studies.

The initiative is designed to help employers navigate the complexities of medication coverage while ensuring employees receive the necessary support and oversight.

By allowing members to purchase medications independently through cash-pay channels, Omada Health provides a financially sustainable pathway for employers to support their workforce’s health needs.

Earnings Surpass Expectations

Omada Health on Thursday reported fourth-quarter earnings of 8 cents per share, beating the consensus of a loss of 3 cents.

Sales reached $75.85 million, up 58% year over year, beating the consensus of $69.67 million.

Omada said it supported more than 150,000 members on GLP-1s, compared with more than 50,000 at the end of 2024

“We demonstrated GLP‑1 companion support innovation, advanced our member‑facing AI capabilities, and introduced meaningful program expansions—all designed to support our members in achieving durable health improvements…,” said Sean Duffy, co‑founder and CEO of Omada Health

Omada Health forecasts fiscal 2026 sales of $312 million-$322 million versus the consensus of $311.41 million.

Omada Health Technical Analysis

The stock is currently trading 31.6% above its 20-day simple moving average (SMA) and 11.5% above its 50-day SMA, indicating short-term strength.

However, it is trading 9.1% below its 100-day SMA, suggesting some longer-term weakness.

The RSI is at 56.70, which is considered neutral territory, indicating that the stock is neither overbought nor oversold. Meanwhile, MACD is at -0.2761, with the signal line at -0.6547, suggesting a bullish momentum as the MACD is above the signal line.

The combination of neutral RSI and bullish MACD suggests mixed momentum, indicating that while the stock is not in an overbought condition, there is potential for upward movement.

  • Key Resistance: $17.00
  • Key Support: $14.50

Analyst Consensus & Recent Actions: The stock carries a Buy Rating with an average price target of $24.18. Recent analyst moves include:

  • Barclays: Overweight (Lowers Target to $17.00) (Mar. 3)
  • Needham: Buy (Maintains Target to $28.00) (Jan. 22)
  • Wells Fargo: Initiated with Equal-Weight (Target $17.00) (Jan. 9)

OMDA Price Action: Omada Health shares were up 10.51% at $15.03 during premarket trading on Friday, according to Benzinga Pro data.

Photo by David Esser via Shutterstock

data.title: Labor Market Shock: US Economy Lost 92,000 Jobs Last Month
data.description:
The U.S. economy lost 92,000 nonfarm payrolls in February 2026, according to data released Friday by the Bureau of Labor Statistics.

The figure marked a sharp slowdown from January's downwardly revised 126,000 job gains and came well below economists' expectations of 59,000.

The unemployment rate surprisingly ticked up from 4.3% to 4.4%, above forecasts of 4.3%.

Meanwhile, average hourly earnings rose 0.4% month-over-month, matching January's pace and topping consensus estimates of 0.3%.

[This is a developing story]

Photo: Shutterstock
